title: SAP bets $1.16B on 18-month-old German AI lab and says yes to NemoClaw
summary: SAP plans to buy German AI startup Prior Labs and invest heavily in it. It is also prohibiting customers' agents use to a select few like Nvidia's NemoClaw.

title: Bumble’s paying users are slipping as it bets on an overhaul later this year
summary: The company is making a big bet that the swiping model is outdated and most matches never turn into actual dates. The company wants to fix that by redesigning profiles, changing how people interact, and focusing a lot more on getting users to meet in real life.

title: Altara secures $7M to bridge the data gap that’s slowing down physical sciences
summary: Altara’s AI aims to diagnose failures and help speed up R&D by unifying  data siloed across spreadsheets and legacy systems.

title: Lucid Motors doesn’t know how many EVs it will build this year
summary: Lucid Motors pulled its guidance for the year, as it navigates swelling inventory and a companywide cost-cutting measure.

title: As crypto cools, a16z crypto raises a $2.2B fund
summary: As some of the biggest VCs in crypto start to consider funding AI startups, a16z crypto's new fund will stay the course.

title: Apple plans to make iOS 27 a Choose Your Own Adventure of AI models
summary: With Apple's latest operating system updates, users will reportedly have their pick of which third-party AI models they want to use for a host of tasks.

title: ASML CEO Christophe Fouquet on his company’s monopoly: no one is coming for us
summary: Christophe Fouquet, who became ASML's CEO in 2024 after more than a decade at the company, sat down with this editor on the rooftop deck of his Beverly Hills hotel Tuesday morning ahead of his appearance at the Milken Institute Global Conference. Dressed in a blue suit and white shirt, he was relaxe

title: Volkswagen becomes Rivian’s top shareholder, displacing Amazon
summary: VW Group's stake will continue to grow under the $5.8 billion joint venture with Rivian.

title: Threads finally brings messaging to the web
summary: By bringing messaging to the web, Threads is aligning its desktop experience more closely with competitors like X and Bluesky.

title: Airbnb co-founder taps Peter Arnell as first US chief brand architect
summary: Arnell joins Gebbia’s National Design Studio under Trump to unify UX across 27,000 federal sites and streamline how Americans use government services.

title: Pennsylvania sues Character.AI after a chatbot allegedly posed as a doctor
summary: According to Pennsylvania's filing, a Character.AI chatbot presented itself as a licensed psychiatrist during a state investigation, and also fabricated a serial number for its state medical license.

title: OpenAI releases GPT-5.5 Instant, a new default model for ChatGPT
summary: The company said the model reduces hallucination in sensitive areas such as law, medicine, and finance, while maintaining the low latency of its predecessor.

title: PayPal says it’s ‘becoming a technology company again’ — that means AI
summary: PayPal is pitching an AI-led turnaround, tying automation and restructuring to $1.5 billion in savings as it cuts jobs and works to modernize its tech stack.

title: Etsy launches its app within ChatGPT as it continues its AI push
summary: Etsy's new native app within ChatGPT aims to be a conversational  shopping experience for users.

title: Kaspersky suspects Chinese hackers planted a backdoor into Daemon Tools in ‘widespread’ attack
summary: The cybersecurity company says it's seen thousands of infection attempts, and at least a dozen successful hacks after users installed malicious versions of the popular Windows software.

title: Hackers steal students’ data during breach at education tech giant Instructure
summary: The data breach at education tech giant Instructure includes students' private data, according to a sample of the allegedly stolen data seen by TechCrunch.

title: Meta will use AI to analyze height and bone structure to identify if users are underage
summary: The visual analysis system is now operating in select countries, but Meta says it's working toward a broader rollout.

title: ElevenLabs lists BlackRock, Jamie Foxx, and Eva Longoria as new investors
summary: ElevenLabs reveals new investors, hits $500M ARR, and expands enterprise footprint as voice AI becomes a critical interface.

title: CopilotKit raises $27M to help devs deploy app-native AI agents
summary: The Seattle-based startup's Series A round was led by Glilot Capital, NFX, and SignalFire, TechCrunch has exclusively learned.

title: 4 days left: Get 50% off a second TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 pass to make more deals faster
summary: For the next four days only, you can buy one pass to TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 and get 50% off a second of the same ticket type. That window closes May 8 at 11:59 p.m. PT. After that, prices go up, and you’ll pay more to bring a partner or colleague. Register today to get your plus-one pass at 50% off

title: OpenAI president forced to read his personal diary entries to jury
summary: Elon Musk argued the journals show the moment when OpenAI abandoned its mission.

title: Silicon Valley bets $200M on AI data centers floating in the ocean
summary: Panthalassa aims to test floating AI computing nodes in the Pacific in 2026.

title: Character.AI sued over chatbot that claims to be a real doctor with a license
summary: State says chatbot claimed to practice medicine, gave invalid license number.

title: Widely used Daemon Tools disk app backdoored in monthlong supply-chain attack
summary: Daemon Tools users: It's time to check your machines for stealthy infections, stat.

title: RFK Jr. plans to curb antidepressants, which he falsely compares to heroin
summary: Kennedy has made—and continues to make—many false claims about SSRIs.

title: Google Home gets upgraded Gemini voice assistant and new camera controls
summary: Google's smart home ecosystem is getting its biggest update since the AI-fueled 2025 revamp.

title: Trump SEC lets Musk settle $150 million Twitter lawsuit for $1.5 million
summary: SEC alleged Musk's late disclosure cheated Twitter investors out of $150 million.

title: How do you design a $30,000 electric pickup? Inside Ford's skunkworks.
summary: We tour Ford’s top-secret Electric Vehicle Development Center in California.

title: Charlize Theron is a bewitching Circe in Odyssey trailer
summary: "You're a man who needs to control his fate. But you cannot control this."

title: Musk's Europe gamble: Will others follow the Dutch and approve FSD?
summary: The Dutch road authority will ask other EU regulators to approve the driver assist.
